items.headline: Iran rejects latest ceasefire proposal as Trump’s deadline approaches
items.summary: Iran on Monday rejected a 45-day ceasefire proposal and said it wants a permanent end to the war, even as Israel attacked a major gas field and U.S. President Donald Trump’s ultimatum to open the Strait of Hormuz loomed. “We only accept an end of the war with…

items.headline: Trump confirms receiving Iran's proposal to end war, says "not good enough"
items.summary: America's President Donald Trump received a proposal from Iran to end the West Asia conflict. He called it a significant step but not good enough. Iran rejected a US ceasefire deal. Instead, Tehran unveiled its own 10-point plan. This plan aims for a permanen…

items.headline: Tehran rejects latest ceasefire proposal, wants permanent end to the war: Iran's state media
items.summary: Tehran rejects the ceasefire proposal, demanding a permanent end to the war with guarantees against future attacks, reports state media.

items.headline: US, Iran Study Ceasefire Plan as Deadline Nears on Trump’s ‘Hell’ Threat
items.summary: The United States and Iran on Monday were weighing the framework of a plan to end their five‑week-old conflict, even as Tehran pushed back against pressure to swiftly reopen the Strait of Hormuz on the eve of a new ultimatum …

items.headline: U.S., Iran Study Ceasefire Plan As Trump’s ‘Hell’ Warning Nears Deadline
items.summary: The U.S. and Iran on Monday were weighing the framework of a plan to end their five‑week-old conflict, even as Tehran pushed back against pressure to swiftly reopen the Strait of Hormuz on the eve of a new ultimatum set by Trump.

items.headline: Wall Street’s Eyes on the Strait
items.summary: With markets wobbling and bad information everywhere, Citrini Research sent an analyst to the war zone to figure out what’s really happening.

items.headline: Iran war: What is happening on day 37 of US-Israeli attacks?
items.summary: A US crew member who has been missing since Iran shot down a fighter jet has been rescued, President Donald Trump says.

items.headline: Japanese, French and Omani vessels cross Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Three Omani-operated tankers, a French-owned container ship and a Japanese-owned gas carrier have crossed the Strait ‌of Hormuz since Thursday, shipping data showed, reflecting Iran's policy to allow passage for vessels it deems friendly. Iran initially shut …

items.headline: 'Regime change' in the Strait of Hormuz sees 20,000 sailors stranded at sea
items.summary: Interviews and first-hand accounts from sailors stranded in the Persian Gulf — under threat of drone attacks, missiles, and exploding boats — reveal an increasingly dire humanitarian crisis unfolding.

items.headline: What is Benjamin Netanyahu’s end game in the Iran war?
items.summary: The war in the Middle East is a huge political book for the Israeli prime minister in an election year – but it may come at a great cost.

items.headline: Sign Of The Times
items.summary: If you haven’t seen the movie, Project Hail Mary, then you should… it is excellent. It is faithful to Andy Weir’s 2021 book, hilarious and heart-warming, and with the Artemis II crew circling the moon, the film is just what we needed.

items.headline: Will China join Pakistan-led efforts to mediate US-Iran peace?
items.summary: Pakistan's Deputy PM Ishaq Dar visits China after Islamabad talks to broker negotiations between the US and Iran.

items.headline: Oil Price Shocks Make Brazil Key to Energy Security in the Americas
items.summary: Oil price shocks since the U.S. and Israel first struck Iran, the world’s fifth-largest oil producer, have global markets on edge. The sudden energy crisis, which emerged after Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz, preventing the passage of around a fifth of the …

items.headline: Current price of oil as of April 6, 2026
items.summary: When oil prices change, it affects your energy costs—and even the price of everyday items. Here’s why.

items.headline: Russia Oil Revenues Halved in March Before War Boost
items.summary: Russian producers paid 494.9 billion rubles ($6.18 billion) in oil taxes last month, down 48 percent year on year, according to Bloomberg calculations based on Finance Ministry data released Friday.

items.headline: Russia’s Oil Revenues Halved in March Before Iran War Boost
items.summary: Russia’s oil tax revenue in March dropped by nearly half compared to a year earlier, highlighting the Kremlin’s financial strain just before the Middle East war delivered an unexpected boost to Moscow’s earnings.

items.headline: Oil prices surge while Asian share prices rise moderately
items.summary: Oil prices spike amid Iran war concerns as Asian markets show modest gains in cautious trading.

items.headline: Trump continues talks despite Iran downing two US jets; ceasefire odds drop to 1.1%
items.summary:
Market skepticism highlights the fragile state of US-Iran relations, with diplomatic efforts struggling to gain traction amid rising tensions.

items.headline: Iran’s former diplomat calls for US deal to end conflict as ceasefire markets dip
items.summary:
The call for US-Iran negotiations highlights potential diplomatic shifts, but market skepticism suggests limited immediate impact.

items.summary:

items.summary: Editor-in-Chief Atul Singh and Simon Cleobury, Head of Arms Control and Disarmament at the Geneva Centre for Security Policy, discuss the expiration of the New Strategic Arms Reduction Treaty (New START) and what its disappearance means for global nuclear sta…

items.headline: Deterrent or danger: After Iran, are Saudi Arabia and Turkey next in line for nuclear weapons?
items.summary: The nuclear threat in the Middle East is growing due to the Iran war, and not just because nuclear sites in Iran and Israel are being attacked. Other countries may decide they're safer with a nuclear weapon too.

items.headline: Ukraine Steps Up Drone Attacks on Russian Oil Infrastructure
items.summary: The key Russian oil export terminal at Novorossiysk was on fire early on Monday following an overnight drone attack, as Ukraine intensifies strikes on Russia’s oil infrastructure to reduce its export capabilities amid surging oil prices. The area at the port …

items.headline: Three killed in Russian strike on Odesa as Ukraine hits Russian oil sites
items.summary: Russian drone strikes in Odesa claimed civilian lives including a toddler. Ukrainian drones retaliated targeting Russia's oil export ports.  The attacks damaged infrastructure and injured civilians. Ukraine faces ongoing challenges with air defense.  Russia c…

items.headline: Russia Expands Gasoline Export Ban, Faces Major Oil Outages Due to Drone Strikes
items.summary: Russia Bans Gasoline Exports, Confirms Major Baltic Oil Terminal Outages The Russian government has expanded a ban on gasoline exports to include producers of the fuel, according to a report from Interfax news agency [1]. The ban, which now includes all gasol…

items.headline: Ukraine Hits Russian Oil Facilities, Says At Least 1 Civilian Killed In Russian Strikes
items.summary: Ukraine has kept up its attacks on Russian oil export and production infrastructure, hitting facilities in two regions, while widespread Russian drone attacks on Ukraine killed at least one civilian overnight and fighting persisted at the front, officials sai…

items.headline: US Search For Missing Crew Member Continues, As Strike Reported Near Iran Nuclear Plant
items.summary: Rescue teams are continuing to search for a US crew member missing after their fighter jet was brought down over Iran, as reports emerged of a strike near the Bushehr nuclear power plant.

items.headline: Trump's 'English Obscenities' to Iran - Latest War News
items.summary: US President Donald Trump has warned Iran that if an agreement is not reached within the next 48 hours, he will launch severe attacks on Iran's energy and transportation infrastructure, forcing Iran to allow ship traffic through the Strait of Hormuz. Trump, u…

items.headline: Satellite images begin to show damage wrought by Iran war
items.summary: Satellite images are emerging, offering a view of the Iran war's damage. Ships are seen burning in an Iranian port. Military bases in the region show destroyed buildings. Information on the war's toll has been limited. High-resolution images from various firm…

items.headline: IAEA Reports Iran Has Not Provided Access to Nuclear Facilities Since March, Warns of Possible Radiological Release
items.summary: IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi warned of potential evacuations as large as major cities if nuclear facilities are hit. Iran has not provided access to affected nuclear facilities or reports since June 2025 attacks, while maintaining storage at underground Isfahan complex.

dimension_signal.rationale: Warned cannot rule out radiological release with serious consequences requiring major city evacuations

items.headline: Pakistan Secures Initial IMF Approval for $1.2 Billion Emergency Loan Amid Iran War Economic Pressures
items.summary: Pakistan reached staff-level agreement with IMF to unlock $1.2 billion from $7 billion bailout program as geopolitical risks from Middle East conflict threaten economic recovery. Country faces three-front security challenges while managing debt obligations.

items.headline: Turkey Explores New Mediation Venues After Pakistan-Led Iran-US Talks Collapse
items.summary: Iran told mediators it will not meet US officials in Islamabad and rejected US demands as unacceptable. Turkey and Egypt now exploring Doha or Istanbul venues with fresh proposals to break deadlock between Washington and Tehran.

dimension_signal.rationale: Actively pursuing diplomatic mediation role to prevent regional war escalation

items.headline: China and Pakistan Unveil Five-Point Initiative for Iran-US Ceasefire, Propose Strait of Hormuz Reopening
items.summary: China and Pakistan presented joint peace initiative calling for immediate ceasefire and Strait of Hormuz reopening. Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi made 26 phone calls to regional parties, while Beijing positions itself as diplomatic mediator.

items.headline: India-Pakistan Exchange Daily Fire Along LOC for Eighth Consecutive Night Following Pahalgam Attack
items.summary: Pakistani troops violated ceasefire along Line of Control for eighth consecutive night after April 22 Pahalgam terrorist attack that killed 26 civilians. Indian army retaliated in calibrated manner, marking most intense breakdown since 2021 ceasefire agreement.

items.headline: Kim Jong-un Oversees Engine Test for Solid-Fuel ICBM Capable of Reaching US Mainland
items.summary: North Korean leader observed test of upgraded solid-fuel engine with 2,500 kilonewton thrust using carbon fiber materials. Kim called test significant for putting country's strategic military muscle on highest level, part of five-year arms buildup plan.

dimension_signal.rationale: Boasted that countries attacked by tested weapons cannot survive while showcasing military prowess

items.headline: ICC Arrest Warrant Against Putin Remains Active Despite Mongolia Visit, Enforcement Challenges Continue
items.summary: International Criminal Court arrest warrant issued March 2023 against Vladimir Putin for unlawful deportation of Ukrainian children remains binding in 125 member states. Putin traveled to Mongolia in September 2024 without detention despite legal obligations under Rome Statute.

items.headline: Netanyahu Coalition Advances Bill to Cancel Criminal Charges PM Faces, Opposition Calls It 'Coup'
items.summary: Coalition lawmakers advance bill to repeal fraud and breach of trust crimes Netanyahu faces in corruption trial. Opposition Leader Yair Lapid called it full-fledged coup that would turn Israel into failed third-world country. Bill text unclear on retroactive application.

dimension_signal.rationale: Coalition advancing legislation to retroactively cancel criminal charges he faces in corruption trial

items.headline: Iran Ambassador to Pakistan Says Mediation Efforts Approaching 'Critical, Sensitive Stage'
items.summary: Iranian Ambassador Reza Amiri Moghadam stated Pakistan's mediation efforts to stop the war are approaching critical and sensitive stage. China supports Pakistan's mediation role while Tehran maintains conditions for permanent ceasefire and guarantees against renewed aggression.

items.headline: Iran's Defense Council Declares Strait of Hormuz Passage Depends on Coordination with Tehran for Non-Belligerent Countries
items.summary: Iran's Defense Council announced passage through Strait of Hormuz would depend on coordination with Tehran for countries described as non-belligerent. Statement comes amid Trump's threats to target Iranian energy infrastructure if strait remains closed.
